concept: Contraindicated / Advised Against Under a Stated Clinical Reason
description: Advised against under a stated clinical reason, by a stated source. The word reports advice, not impossibility: a contraindication can be absolute, relative, or temporary, and naming which one keeps the door open to ask about alternatives.
sound_symbolism: Dental 'na' starts with a steady nasal contact and broad 'a'. Stressed 'tho' keeps the tongue forward but turns resonance into breath, rounded in 'o'. Final 'ku' closes firmly at the velum and sinks into deep 'u'. Two kinds of dental contact lead to the last hard stop.
grammatical_notes: An adjective: 'ha nepha nathoku nai.' (This medicine is contraindicated.) State the person or condition, intervention, source, reason, strength, alternatives, and uncertainty where relevant. The quality-noun rule supplies contraindication. Distinct from 'peloma' (harmful), 'whemaki' (allergy), 'naweri' (refuse), 'kanuwa' (authorize), and practical impossibility.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A sourced contraindication can guide caution without erasing the person's choice or pretending that one clinical reason settles every context.

tags.cognition: a source-bounded judgment against a proposed intervention
tags.physical: a clinical relation between an intervention and bodily conditions
